steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: To the left is an introductory prepositional phrase (“In a JSON file storing data about fire belly newts”), ending with a noun. To the right is the transitional phrase “for instance” followed by an independent clause (“you could encounter a key such as...”).

steps.title: Apply the parenthetical punctuation rule
steps.content:
A parenthetical phrase is set apart from surrounding text by commas on both sides. Choice B correctly uses a comma before “for instance,” treating it as parenthetical within a single sentence. The other choices violate basic punctuation rules: A creates a sentence fragment, C misuses a colon meant for introducing elaboration, and D applies a semicolon when the left side is not an independent clause.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
